appeal_letter:
Respectfully,

Re: Appeal of denial for Bilateral Reduction Mammaplasty (breast reduction)

I am appealing the denial of the requested Bilateral Reduction Mammaplasty.

The denial, issued under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin 0017, states that the request is denied because the clinical information provided does not demonstrate that conservative treatment has been exhausted. The denial explains that the policy requires documentation of at least three consecutive months of conservative therapy, such as medically supervised physical therapy, chiropractic care, or specialized support garments, that failed to relieve symptoms, and that the records provided only mention over-the-counter NSAIDs. On that basis the surgery was characterized as cosmetic and not medically necessary.

The basis for this appeal is that the conservative therapy requirement has in fact been met, and the denial rested on an incomplete record rather than on the absence of such treatment. I have used conservative measures for longer than the policy's stated threshold: I have been seeing a massage therapist for six months and have been wearing custom support bras, while continuing to experience severe grooving in the shoulders, chronic upper back pain, and recurring rashes under the breasts. These measures span well beyond the three consecutive months the policy describes and have not relieved the documented symptoms. The reason the prior authorization submission reflected only over-the-counter NSAIDs is that the receipts and clinical notes for the massage therapy and custom support garments were not included in the original submission by the provider's office. The criterion the denial relied upon therefore supports the request once the omitted documentation is considered, because that documentation shows sustained conservative therapy that failed to resolve the condition.

To the extent the policy weighs whether each specific listed modality was used, I ask that the individualized clinical facts be considered: a documented six-month course of conservative therapy together with persistent shoulder grooving, chronic upper back pain, and recurring submammary rashes reflects a functional, symptom-driven condition rather than a cosmetic one.

I also note that, while the denial states an appeal may be filed within 180 days, it does not describe the process for submitting this appeal or affirm the right to a full and fair review of the complete record. I request a complete and fair review that takes into account the conservative-therapy documentation referenced above.

I request that the denial be overturned and the Bilateral Reduction Mammaplasty be authorized, and that the conservative-therapy documentation be reviewed once provided. I am working to obtain and submit the supporting receipts and clinical notes for the massage therapy and custom support garments.

Not legal or medical advice. Draft assistance only.

Respectfully,
Member
missing_evidence_checklist: Clinical notes from the six months of massage therapy documenting dates, treatment, and response
Receipts or fitting records for the custom support bras showing dates of use
Treating physician's notes documenting shoulder grooving, chronic upper back pain, and recurring submammary rashes and their persistence despite conservative care
Any documentation tying the six-month conservative course to the policy's three-month consecutive-therapy requirement
Aetna's specific instructions or form for submitting this appeal, and confirmation of the right to a full and fair review
